stories.title: COS Childcare Desert — City Council Approves Zoning Fix
stories.angle: El Paso County has 47,000 children under five and only 18,400 licensed childcare spots. The Colorado Springs City Council approved a zoning ordinance that allows home-based and commercial childcare as a permitted use in residential zones — the most significant policy change in years for COS families navigating the childcare gap. The southeast quadrant has the fewest spots per child. Today's story explains what changed, why it matters, and what families can realistically expect next.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
COS has 47,000 kids under 5 — only 18,400 licensed childcare spots. 🏫

City Council approved a zoning fix: childcare can now open in homes, businesses, and churches. Southeast COS has it worst.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
New COS zoning: home daycares, church-based care, and employer childcare are now permitted in residential zones.

28,600 missing spots in El Paso County. This doesn't fix everything — but the biggest barrier is gone.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you've ever tried to find childcare in Colorado Springs and come up empty, there's a reason for that — and it has a name. A childcare desert is what happens when the number of kids who need care far outstrips the number of licensed spots available. In El Paso County, that gap is 28,600 spots wide. The county has 47,000 children under the age of five and only 18,400 licensed childcare spots to go around. 🏫

The Colorado Springs City Council has approved a zoning ordinance that changes how childcare facilities can operate in the city. Under the previous rules, opening a childcare center in a home, church, or business where it served a secondary purpose wasn't allowed. The new ordinance makes home-based care, church-based care, and employer-provided childcare permitted uses in residential zones. That removes the biggest policy-level barrier that's kept new spots from opening.

The southeast quadrant of Colorado Springs has the fewest available spots relative to the children who live there. If you're on the east side or the southeast side, you've already felt this firsthand. The ordinance won't immediately fill 28,600 spots — that's not how zoning works — but it removes the legal wall that was blocking providers from opening where families need them most.

This is a real step. We'll keep watching to see how many new providers take advantage of the change and where they set up.

What's your experience been finding childcare in COS? Drop a comment — we're curious whether families have found it harder in certain parts of the city.
facebook.image_caption: 47,000 kids. 18,400 childcare spots. Colorado Springs just changed its zoning to help close that gap. Home daycares, church-based care, and employer childcare are now permitted in residential zones. 🏫

article.headline: Colorado Springs Approves Zoning Fix for Childcare Desert

stories.title: Free Concert at Piñon Valley Park — July 24
stories.angle: The SofaKillers play Piñon Valley Park (5585 Mule Deer Dr) on Thursday July 24, 6:30–8:30 PM, as part of Park and Recreation Month. Free admission, food trucks, youth kickball, and giveaways. Bring lawn chairs and blankets. Part of the city's Music in the Park series.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Free Thursday night for COS families 🎶

SofaKillers live at Piñon Valley Park (5585 Mule Deer Dr) — July 24, 6:30–8:30 PM. Food trucks, kickball, giveaways. Bring lawn chairs.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Thursday just got a lot better for COS families. 🎶

The SofaKillers are playing Piñon Valley Park this Thursday, July 24, from 6:30 to 8:30 PM. The park is at 5585 Mule Deer Dr, and the whole evening is free. Bring lawn chairs, blankets, and your whole crew — there's no admission charge.

While the music plays, there are food trucks on site, giveaways, and youth kickball happening alongside the concert. It's one of those evenings where you show up expecting a concert and end up with a whole evening of things to do. This is part of the city's Park and Recreation Month celebrations for July.

Pack snacks, pack blankets, pack your patience for wrangling kids around food trucks, and head over Thursday evening.

Will you be there? Tell us in the comments — and tag a family you'd bring along!
facebook.image_caption: 🎶 Free concert Thursday! SofaKillers at Piñon Valley Park (5585 Mule Deer Dr) — July 24, 6:30–8:30 PM. Food trucks, kickball, giveaways. Bring lawn chairs. Free for all ages.

article.headline: Free Concert at Piñon Valley Park This Thursday: SofaKillers, Food Trucks, and Kickball

stories.title: D49 Back-to-School Countdown — Teachers Return July 28
stories.angle: Falcon School District 49 (D49) begins its return-to-school sequence July 24 with new teacher orientation. All D49 teachers return July 28. The first day of school for kindergartners, 6th graders, and 9th graders is August 3. All students return August 4. A back-to-school planning post for D49 families.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
D49 back-to-school dates 📅

July 24: New teacher orientation
July 28: All teachers return
Aug 3: First day — K, 6th, 9th grade
Aug 4: First day — all students

Falcon School District 49 families, it's almost time.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
For Falcon School District 49 families, summer isn't quite over — but the countdown has officially started. 📅

Here are the key D49 back-to-school dates to put on your calendar right now. New teacher orientation kicks off this Thursday, July 24. Teachers officially return on July 28, with professional development on July 30. The first day of school for kindergartners, sixth graders, and ninth graders is Monday, August 3. All students are back on Tuesday, August 4.

That means most D49 families have about two weeks to get everything sorted. School supplies, first-day outfits, fall schedule adjustments, before- and after-school care — it all comes at once.

The D49 calendar is available at d49.org if you need to check specific school start times or supply lists for individual schools.

Is your family ready for August? What's still on the to-do list?
facebook.image_caption: D49 back-to-school dates: teacher orientation July 24, teachers return July 28, first day (K, 6th, 9th) Aug 3, all students Aug 4. Get the full calendar at d49.org. 📅

article.headline: D49 Back-to-School 2026: Key Dates for Falcon School District Families

stories.title: NICU Parent Mental Health — JAMA Pediatrics Study
stories.angle: A July 6, 2026 meta-analysis in JAMA Pediatrics — 160 studies, 16,639 parents — found that psychotherapy, family-centered care, and meditation significantly reduce stress and anxiety in parents of NICU infants. Led by Claire E. Niehaus Milligan, PhD, Cincinnati Children's Hospital. Effect sizes were large to extremely large. Directly useful for any COS family who has navigated a NICU stay.

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
Psychotherapy, family-centered care, and meditation reduce stress and anxiety in NICU parents. 💙

New JAMA Pediatrics meta-analysis — 160 studies, 16,639 parents. Effect sizes: large to extremely large.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
If your baby spent time in the NICU, your mental health deserves the same attention as theirs did.

A July 2026 JAMA Pediatrics meta-analysis confirmed the interventions work. Ask your NICU team about support resources.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your family spent time in the NICU, you already know what the research has now confirmed: it's one of the hardest things a parent can go through. 💙

A meta-analysis published in JAMA Pediatrics on July 6, 2026 pulled together 160 studies involving 16,639 parents to look at what kinds of support actually help during a NICU stay. The findings are clear: psychotherapy and emotional support, family-centered care and education, and meditation and holistic approaches all produced significant reductions in parental stress and anxiety. Psychotherapy showed the most consistent pattern of benefit across all mental health outcomes. Effect sizes across the study ranged from large to extremely large — which means these weren't marginal benefits.

The study was led by Claire E. Niehaus Milligan, PhD, of the Heart Mind Wellbeing Center at Cincinnati Children's Hospital Medical Center. The six categories of intervention included bonding approaches, expressive and creative therapies, psychotherapy, family-centered care, meditation and holistic approaches, and spiritual interventions.

What this means practically: if you're in a NICU right now, or you know someone who is, asking the care team about mental health support isn't optional — it's part of the care. And if your NICU stay is in the past and the stress stayed with you after discharge, that's worth naming and treating too.

What resources helped your family during a NICU stay? If you're comfortable sharing, we'd love to hear what made a difference.
facebook.image_caption: A July 2026 JAMA Pediatrics meta-analysis: 160 studies, 16,639 parents. Psychotherapy, family-centered care, and meditation all significantly reduce NICU parent stress and anxiety. Support works. 💙

article.headline: Study Confirms Mental Health Support Works for NICU Parents — and Tells Us What Works Best

stories.title: PPLD Summer Reading — 10 Days Left, Grand Prize Is Broadmoor Cloud Camp
stories.angle: Pikes Peak Library District's Summer Adventure program ends July 31 — 10 days from today. Grand prizes include a Pikes Peak Region Attractions VIP Access Pass and a one-night stay for two at The Broadmoor Cloud Camp. Free, all ages. Complete 30 days of reading, creating, and exploring to earn entries. Register or log activities at ppld.org/summer.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
10 days left — PPLD Summer Adventure ends July 31. 📚

Grand prize: Pikes Peak Attractions pass + one night at The Broadmoor Cloud Camp.

Log activity at ppld.org/summer. Free for all ages.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
There are 10 days left in PPLD's Summer Adventure program, and if you haven't finished yet — or haven't started — now is the moment. 📚

The Pikes Peak Library District Summer Adventure runs through July 31. The program is free for all ages, and the goal is simple: complete 30 days of reading, creating, or exploring to earn entries into prize drawings. Prizes range from local attraction passes and gift cards to the grand prize: a Pikes Peak Region Attractions VIP Access Pass and a one-night stay for two at The Broadmoor Cloud Camp.

You can log your reading and activities at ppld.org/summer. If your kids have been keeping up all summer, a few more days of logging could put them in the running for something really memorable. If you're starting fresh today, you've still got time.

The program wraps on July 31. Don't let the summer end without cashing in on one of the best free programs in Colorado Springs.

Have your kids been participating in Summer Adventure? Drop a comment — we'd love to hear what they've been reading!
facebook.image_caption: 📚 10 days left! PPLD Summer Adventure ends July 31. Grand prize: Pikes Peak Attractions pass + a night at The Broadmoor Cloud Camp. Free for all ages. Log at ppld.org/summer.

article.headline: 10 Days Left: PPLD Summer Adventure Ends July 31 — Grand Prize Is a Night at The Broadmoor Cloud Camp
